text.title: Docker Compose Examples
text.summary: Full-stack compose example with dev/prod override pattern, watch live-reload block, and common antipatterns.

section.title: Full-Stack Web Application
section.p: Production-ready stack: app + PostgreSQL + Redis + Nginx + optional Celery worker. All secrets via .env, internal backend network, healthchecks on all depends_on targets.

example.note: Worker is profile-gated; starts only with `docker compose --profile worker up -d`.

section.title: Dev/Prod Override Pattern
section.p: `docker-compose.override.yml` is auto-loaded in development. `docker-compose.prod.yml` is merged explicitly with `-f`.

example.code: # docker-compose.override.yml (dev, auto-loaded) services: app: build: target: development volumes: - .:/app - /app/node_modules environment: DEBUG: "true" ports: - "9229:9229" # debug port db: ports: - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432" # expose for local tools redis: ports: - "127.0.0.1:6379:6379"

# docker-compose.prod.yml (explicit: -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.yml) services: app: image: registry.example.com/myapp:${VERSION} build: null # never build in production restart: always deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 resources: limits: {memory: 1G, cpus: "2.0"}

section.title: Live Reload with docker compose watch
section.p: Available in Compose v2.22+. Replaces volume bind-mount hot reload with explicit sync rules.

example.code: services: app: develop: watch: - action: sync path: ./src target: /app/src ignore: [node_modules/, __pycache__/] - action: rebuild path: package.json - action: sync+restart path: ./config target: /app/config
example.note: Run with `docker compose watch` (not `up`). Sync does not restart; rebuild recreates the image; sync+restart syncs then gracefully restarts the container.

antipattern.reason: `docker compose config` and `docker inspect` reveal these values to anyone with Docker socket access.
Provides only startup ordering, not readiness. The dependent service starts before the database is accepting connections.

antipattern.description: Building images in production compose files.
antipattern.reason: Build context includes dev files (.git, test data). Use pre-built images from a registry in production.
Docker inserts an ACCEPT iptables rule before UFW rules, exposing the port to the internet regardless of firewall settings.
